dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 2 for an unknown problem/person down at the foot of the steps in [CITY].
Hi, I'm Mark, an EMT with the rescue squad. We're responding to a person down call - can you tell us what's happening here?
He's here, unresponsive, with very slow breathing. We called when we found him like this, maybe 5 minutes ago.
Sir, can you hear me? Can you open your eyes for me?
Eyes remain closed. No eye opening despite verbal stimuli.
No verbal response. Motor response cannot be assessed at this time.
The patient is unresponsive, consistent with a Glasgow Coma Scale of 3, as reported by field staff prior to our arrival.
The airway is patent following a jaw-thrust maneuver by first responders for sonorous respirations.
Respiratory rate initially was 6 to 8 per minute, and assisted breathing via BVM was initiated due to inadequate respiration.
The radial pulse is strong and present initially, though initial BP could not be obtained.
Pulse is 90 beats per minute, firm and regular during the initial assessment.
Respiratory rate starts at 6 to 8 RPM and later improves to 13 RPM after BVM ventilation.
Initial blood pressure readings were unobtainable but later recorded at 115/72 mmHg after Narcan administration.
Initial blood glucose was unobtainable but was later measured at 107 mg/dL following assisted breathing.
BVM ventilation at 15 LPM with a mask was initiated to assist breathing, and capnography was placed to monitor end-tidal CO2.
Oxygen saturation is initially at 99%, later dropping to 98% post Narcan, with no hypoxia observed.
A 26 Fr nasopharyngeal airway was inserted in the right nostril after lubrication to assist breathing.
A 4-lead EKG was placed and showed normal sinus rhythm.
A 18-gauge IV was placed in the left antecubital fossa with a saline lock for medication access.
After Narcan administration, the patient regains some responsiveness and follows verbal commands to a degree.
The patient was physically restrained with cravats to ensure safety for EMS and the patient before Narcan administration.
Personal information will be collected once the patient is more alert following Narcan.
The patient later admitted heroin use and reported Celexa use for depression.
What medications are you currently taking?
I take Celexa for depression.
Do you have any known drug or environmental allergies?
No known allergies.
When was your last food or drink?
I had dinner a few hours ago, maybe a salad-nothing else.
Tell me about any other medical conditions or history?
I have a diagnosis of major depression-nothing else.
The skin is warm and diaphoretic with no cyanosis.
Pupils are pinpoint and reactive, consistent with opioid overdose findings.
Gross motor and sensory function in all extremities appears intact based on documentation.
Reassessed oxygen saturation remains at 98% with 2 LPM nasal cannula.
EKG shows stable sinus rhythm; second 12-lead tracing was within normal range.
0.25 mg of Narcan was administered IVP following dual verification.
The patient vomited twice post-Narcan, and we repositioned him to assist with drainage.
Glucose was temporarily unattainable post-vomiting but successfully measured later.
Oxygen at 2 LPM via nasal prongs is continued for supplemental support.
Positioning was adjusted to ensure proper 12-lead EKG electrode placement.
The second 12-lead tracing at 1:45:32 shows a normal sinus rhythm from baseline findings.
EMS protocols followed include 3-15 for altered mental status and 7-1 for opioid overdose.
Let's reassess the patient's vitals before preparation for transport.
The pulse has stabilized to 83 BPM and remains normal.
Respiration is now at 12 RPM with no signs of distress.
Blood pressure remains at 114/73 with no new changes in vital signs.
[COMPANY_NAME] is selected for evaluation and monitoring due to opioid overdose risks.
Let me verify that the Reeves stretcher with 5-point restraints is ready for patient movement.
The patient was safely transferred to the RAA stretcher and is secured for transport.
All monitoring devices remain active en route and hospital has been informed.
ED24 bed has been pre-arranged for arrival as per the ePCR.
All interventions are documented and ready for handover upon arrival at the hospital.
